paper_overview.citation: Pinakshi Biswas, Bhanu Prakash Vellanki. Occurrence of emerging contaminants in highly anthropogenically influenced river Yamuna in India. Science of the Total Environment 782 (2021) 146741.
paper_overview.study_region: River Yamuna, Northern India, including its tributaries Hindon river and Hindon canal, covering a distance of 575 km from Dakpatthar to Etawah.

data_sources.description: Summary statistics (frequency of detection, maximum, minimum, and mean concentrations) for 16 emerging contaminants in Yamuna river water during summer and post-monsoon seasons.

Spatiotemporal variations in concentrations (ng/L) of 16 emerging contaminants (Caffeine, Trimethoprim, Ciprofloxacin, Sulfamethoxazole, Enrofloxacin, Testosterone, Progesterone, Estrone, Diclofenac, Naproxen, Ketoprofen, Carbamazepine, Atenolol, Gemfibrozil, Triclosan, 2-hydroxybenzothiazole) at 13 sampling sites during summer and post-monsoon seasons.

Distribution of the number of emerging contaminants (categorized as pharmaceuticals, insecticides, steroids, plasticizers, herbicides, pesticides, fungicides, other industrial chemicals, personal care products) detected in the inlet and outlet of two drinking water treatment plants (Mathura and Agra DWTPs).

sites.description: Dakpatthar, situated close to the origin of Yamuna at the lower Himalayas with a sparse population.
sites.source_quote: The first sampling point Dakpatthar (Y-1) is situated close to the origin of Yamuna at the lower Himalayas with a sparse population.

sites.description: Hindon river, a tributary of Yamuna.
sites.source_quote: Water samples were also collected from Hindon river (Y-2) and Hindon canal (Y-3) which act as the tributaries of Yamuna.

sites.description: Hindon canal, a tributary of Yamuna.
sites.source_quote: Water samples were also collected from Hindon river (Y-2) and Hindon canal (Y-3) which act as the tributaries of Yamuna.

sites.description: Nizamuddin bridge, Delhi, located downstream of the confluence points of major and minor drains with Yamuna.
sites.source_quote: After Y-1, the next three sites were selected in and around the capital, Delhi, including Nizamuddin bridge (Y-4), Okhla barrage (Y-5) and downstream of Yamuna-Shahadara drain confluence (Y-6). The chosen sites in Delhi are situated at the downstream of the confluence points of major and minor drains with Yamuna.

sites.description: Okhla barrage, Delhi, where domestic and industrial waste from Gaziabad city flows into the Yamuna.
sites.source_quote: After Y-1, the next three sites were selected in and around the capital, Delhi, including Nizamuddin bridge (Y-4), Okhla barrage (Y-5) and downstream of Yamuna-Shahadara drain confluence (Y-6). The chosen sites in Delhi are situated at the downstream of the confluence points of major and minor drains with Yamuna. At Y-5, domestic and industrial waste from Gaziabad city flows into the Yamuna.

sites.description: Downstream of Shahadara drain & Yamuna Confluence, approximately 1 km downstream of Shahadara drain.
sites.source_quote: After Y-1, the next three sites were selected in and around the capital, Delhi, including Nizamuddin bridge (Y-4), Okhla barrage (Y-5) and downstream of Yamuna-Shahadara drain confluence (Y-6). The chosen sites in Delhi are situated at the downstream of the confluence points of major and minor drains with Yamuna. Y-6 is approximately 1 km downstream of Shahadara drain and Yamuna confluence.

sites.description: Upstream of Mathura, a town with approximate 442 thousand population.
sites.source_quote: In order to understand the effect of Mathura, a town with approximate 442 thousand population, of great religious importance, samples were collected upstream (Y-7) and downstream of Mathura (Y-8).
In order to understand the effect of Mathura, a town with approximate 442 thousand population, of great religious importance, samples were collected upstream (Y-7) and downstream of Mathura (Y-8).

sites.description: Upstream of Agra city, a town with approximately 1.59 million population and a tourist hotspot.
sites.source_quote: Next three sites were chosen from Agra, a town with approximately 1.59 million population and a tourist hotspot. Samples upstream of Agra (Y-9), Agra city near Taj Mahal (Y-10) and downstream of Agra (Y-11) were collected.

Next three sites were chosen from Agra, a town with approximately 1.59 million population and a tourist hotspot. Samples upstream of Agra (Y-9), Agra city near Taj Mahal (Y-10) and downstream of Agra (Y-11) were collected.

Next three sites were chosen from Agra, a town with approximately 1.59 million population and a tourist hotspot. Samples upstream of Agra (Y-9), Agra city near Taj Mahal (Y-10) and downstream of Agra (Y-11) were collected.
The last two sites were chosen upstream (Y-12) and downstream of Etawah (Y-13).

sites.description: Downstream of Etawah, close to the outfall of the Etawah STP.
sites.source_quote: The last two sites were chosen upstream (Y-12) and downstream of Etawah (Y-13). The site Y-13 is close to the outfall of the Etawah STP.

temporal_coverage.sampling_dates: Summer sampling was conducted in May, and post-monsoon sampling was conducted in September.

extraction_notes: The study quantified 16 target compounds including pharmaceuticals, personal care products, and hormones in surface water samples collected from 13 locations along the Yamuna river and its tributaries (Hindon river and Hindon canal) during summer (May) and post-monsoon (September) seasons. Concentrations are reported in ng/L. Additionally, the number of detected emerging contaminants categorized by type was reported for the inlet and outlet of two drinking water treatment plants (Mathura and Agra DWTPs).
